2606.31594.title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning based Intrusion Detection in Realistic IoT Networks
2606.31594.paper_summary: The Internet of Things (IoT) is rapidly growing and expanding into various sectors, such as healthcare, transportation, smart homes, and more. Despite the benefits of using IoT devices, they present several challenges. Given the significant role these devices play in our lives, it is crucial to address issues related to their security and privacy. These devices are limited in resources, which complicates their security and the protection of the data that they manage. The paper aims to examine intrusion detection systems using the Gotham2025 dataset, generated through the Gotham testbed, which consists of 78 emulated IoT devices utilising various protocols, including MQTT, CoAP, and RTSP, to assist in safeguarding IoT networks from attacks. We conduct a comparative analysis between five machine learning algorithms, including Random Forest, XGBoost, Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, and Deep Neural Network. We demonstrate that the Random Forest Classifier was the top-performing model, achieving an F1-score of 0.99 in classifying attacks.
2606.31594.paper_summary_zh: 物聯網（IoT）正在快速增長並擴展到各個領域，例如醫療保健、交通運輸、智慧家庭等。儘管使用 IoT 設備有其好處，但它們也帶來了幾個挑戰。考慮到這些設備在我們生活中扮演的重要角色，解決與其安全性和隱私相關的問題至關重要。這些設備在資源上受到限制，這使得它們的安全性以及所管理數據的保護變得複雜。本文旨在檢視使用 Gotham2025 數據集的入侵檢測系統，該數據集是通過 Gotham 測試平台生成的，包含 78 個模擬的 IoT 設備，利用包括 MQTT、CoAP 和 RTSP 在內的各種協議，以協助保護 IoT 網絡免受攻擊。我們對五種機器學習算法進行了比較分析，包括隨機森林、XGBoost、邏輯回歸、朴素貝葉斯和深度神經網絡。我們展示了隨機森林分類器是表現最佳的模型，在分類攻擊時達到了 0.99 的 F1 分數。
2606.31594.author: Rana Alharbi et.al.
